inline_comments.message:
Wwe had a lot of issue around String v. Object in this and other APIs I forget what the outcome was but there was a good reason for one over the other
We shouldn't change this without a good reason

Should a a test to cover XML mediatype where possible 
just extend te where table with the additional options
only once for each endpoint ie. only extend one/the most basic test for the endpoint)

For test try to keep the data to a minimum, you can probably do with a one liner XML like the josn on line #70
Also move this statemnt to line #71
as mentioned above you can combine this with an existing test.
change to _* for less maintenance going forward :-)
bit hard to compare, but are there THREE variations now of saveData, why exactly?!
related to my previous comment, did you do this deliberately (overload) for backward compatibility?
keep order of parameters consistent and logical. I think you have this as the first parameter in other methods which I thin is better
good test but can easily combined into one

correct indentation.

In IntellJ: settings, Edito, Code Style., Groovy, Tabs and Indents (first tab), Label Indent 4

The case is that Spring is trying to map JSON/XML input as a HashMap. i'm afraid that will do an invalid mapping (as we want to provide that feature there to use Yang files to generate objects from XML instead of trying to convert them from XML to JSON).
backward compatibility. I didn't want to change other APIs, just provide methods needed for a change I made
yes, looks like some accidentally copy-paste
We agreed in CPS to not use var. Use type
need to think about that... I think it might be the right thing to do alright

did you forget to check in the updated version of this file?
You acknowledged the comments in #5 but no changes?!

yes, sorry about that, I made a push but forget to change fix that comments. Right now I wanted to prepare a demo for tomorrow but found a bug 😮 so I'll push that fix with code fixes and changes requested by Joseph

in the swagger documentation page , somehow this example is not getting rendered.
Below is what we see : 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!-- XML example cannot be generated; root element name is undefined -->
NB: we prefer not to shorten names: document

I assume this method is only used for xml use-case becasue of the xml-root element correct?
suggestion: getFirstChildXmlRoot

this just returns the first child?!
is that OK? You only expecting one I suppose (probably related to the ongoing work for https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-341, i think there is a patch almost ready for that so we need to be carefull and coordinate! (maybe a follow up user story to support multiple nodes in xml format)
if so I think it should be reflected in the name
have you go a test that covers this secnario?

I can see what you are trying to do but what really is being tested here is invalid data (format)
instead of swapping the files I would just test with 2 strings: '{invalid json'  and '<some invalid xml'
(the missing closing } and > are deliberate)
not true for firsttest?! See comment below

in this case the output IS different from the input, so doesn't match what is described on line #33 
I actually don understand what exactly is happening, (unless I study the prod code maybe) SO the point is we need a better test, possibly a separate test for this scenario?!
maybe insert and and: block to separate and describe

@Priyank looks like Swagger has problem with generating xml example for "unknown" schema. We know that xml will come but format of that data is not fixed (of course we check that schema later using Yang but on API level that schema is unknown). Is it ok if we skip example for xml and keep for json?
Oh, yes, it makes sense, if we want to add multiple nodes at one with xml as well we should probably discuss it. How it's going to be wrapped on JSON? On array?

To be honest - no. That is such corner case, because can happen only if input xml data is correct with schema (after adding root node) but for some reason after parse it has only root node. So not really related with user input, but some really unexpected software behaviour
Ok, then sentence wasn't clear, I agree. The case is that if xml data is wrapped by root node (deifned as module in YANG) we shouldn't add anything more
I am happy enough with that

Sure..
Maybe we can keep this example and once we have some workaround we can apply the same here..
I am happy to take a note here and move ahead 😊
this is actually repeated with parseJsonData, can be placed outside?

Well, if I place it one level above, into parseData, there will be as well that code used twice, as there are two parseData methods
public static NormalizedNode parseData(final ContentType contentType, final String nodeData,
                                       final SchemaContext schemaContext)
and
public static NormalizedNode parseData(final ContentType contentType, final String nodeData,
                                       final SchemaContext schemaContext, final String parentNodeXpath)


we can also move it into org.onap.cps.api.impl.CpsDataServiceImpl class, but then we are moving logic outside class.
oh i mean as an instance variable maybe ? :)  see line 75

does this affect the impl?
(I hoep not and then it is OK)
NB: could/should this be in a finally block ?!
As far as I understand both of these variables are used during parsing input process. I'm not sure if declaring them above is thread safe, as we are going to use them for different inputs
no, it's just for showing an example (without that there is no correct example on swagger ui)
